verdict: 2026-07-17 07:22:48 PDT — Divided parallelism with one focused hidden-failure critic is a sound, appropriately minimal first shape, and reusing the existing correctness schema is technically sufficient because separate artifacts already identify the lens. The sketch is not ready to build, however: its process orchestration can accept stale output after a failed critic, its fail-open policy lets an incomplete review reach the merge workflow, and its schema decision contradicts the recorded OPS-12 decision.

findings.title: Bare wait and persistent output paths cannot prove both critics succeeded
findings.locus: Design sketch — HOW / Step 8 concurrent bash block and failure posture
findings.claim: A bare `wait` does not preserve and check both child exit statuses, while both critics write directly to stable artifact paths that may already exist from an earlier round. If a background `codex exec` fails before replacing its file, the post-wait missing/JSON check can accept a stale but valid artifact and record it against the current base and HEAD. This is precisely the kind of silent degradation the new lens is intended to catch, and the orchestration pattern will be copied by future critics.
findings.alternative: Remove or isolate prior outputs before launch, capture each child PID, wait for and record each PID's status explicitly, write each run to a fresh temporary artifact, validate the successful output, and only then atomically promote it to the stable review path. Treat an exit failure and an invalid artifact as the same explicit critic failure.
findings.win: Eliminates the stale-review path, makes both child outcomes observable, and establishes one reliable concurrency invariant for every future lens without adding a dependency.

findings.title: Fail-open allows a review to complete without the required critic
findings.claim: Recording the secondary failure is loud, but proceeding through the correctness menu and `/close` still allows the branch to reach the merge fork without the hidden-failure review required by AC1–AC5. The dedicated critic therefore becomes optional exactly when it fails, undermining the acceptance criteria and creating a cross-cutting precedent for future lenses.
findings.alternative: Hard-stop the review round when either required critic fails after recording which critic failed and how to rerun it. If Thomas wants best-effort advisory critics, make that optional status explicit in the acceptance criteria and ensure `/close` cannot mistake the partial result for a completed review.
findings.win: Removes the incomplete-review-to-merge path and preserves a single invariant: a recorded correctness round contains valid output from every required lens.

findings.title: The schema choice conflicts with the recorded OPS-12 decision
findings.locus: Design sketch — Open question 3, AC2, Non-goals; BACKLOG.md OPS-12
findings.claim: The story requires reuse of unchanged `finding-schema.json`, while OPS-12's decided minimal build says the specialized critic has its own schema plus a `lens`/`source` field so grouping and never-re-raise tracking remain unambiguous. The story labels this as still needing Thomas even though the backlog presents it as settled. Building either contract without reconciling the other would leave two incompatible sources of truth.
findings.alternative: Have Thomas explicitly choose the contract before implementation. The simpler recommendation is to reuse `finding-schema.json` and treat the artifact path plus labelled story section as the lens identity, then amend OPS-12 to record that decision. If lens identity must travel with findings outside those artifacts, instead update the ACs and scope to add the dedicated tagged schema described by OPS-12.
findings.win: Restores one authoritative contract, avoids either an unnecessary schema fork or ambiguous provenance, and prevents future critics from copying inconsistent tracking rules.
